page: Microsoft Azure D32ads_v7 is a Dadsv7-series virtual machine configured with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, the server features 16 physical cores with multithreading enabled running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The processor includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, alongside hardware support for AVX-512 vector extensions, SHA-NI cryptography, and SVM virtualization.

This virtual machine operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and includes 1890 GB of local storage. It is designated as Premium Storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance disk options. No dedicated GPU hardware is included in this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the D32ads_v7 demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core tests, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Multi-core performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core tests, PassMark CPU scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory performance is balanced, with PassMark memory scores in the top 25% and large-block memory bandwidth read/write speeds falling in the middle 50%.

Given its balanced resource allocation and high single-core clock speed, the server offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring a mix of compute density and local storage. It is well-suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, database operations, and lightweight LLM inference tasks, such as prompt processing and text generation for smaller models.
description: The Microsoft Azure D32ads_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dadsv7 family, featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 x86_64 processor running at 4.5 GHz, it delivers 16 physical cores with multithreading and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512. The instance includes 1890 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it lacks a dedicated GPU. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core and single-threaded compression tests, alongside strong multi-core and memory performance in the top 25%. This resource profile makes the D32ads_v7 highly efficient for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, database operations, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32ads_v7 is a Dadsv7 virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 1890 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for general-purpose and database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32ads_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage, optimized for database and web workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring high-frequency AMD EPYC processors, substantial local storage, and top-tier single-core performance for diverse enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor
128.0 GB of system memory and 1890 GB of local storage
Top-tier single-core performance in stress-ng and compression benchmarks
Premium Storage capable configuration running on Microsoft Hyper-V
Optimal for general-purpose applications, databases, and web serving
